commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are the proxy/security SDK contract decision and contributor-supplied current-head proof, not a safe autonomous repair.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive because it adds a managed-proxy direct path for loopback Ollama embeddings and exposes the bypass marker through a public SDK subpath.; Review finding: [P1] Keep the proxy bypass out of the publi... (sha=0aa6bc355fae4268ab2b0bba6dfa66ee02be61a8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a guarded managed-proxy bypass for exact loopback Ollama memory embedding fetches, switches Ollama embedding SSRF trust from hostname to exact origin, updates tests, exports new SDK types, and documents proxy behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main sends strict guarded fetches through EnvHttpProxyAgent when the managed proxy is active, and the Ollama embedding provider passes no bypass option. I did not run a live Ollama/proxy repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are the proxy/security SDK contract decision and contributor-supplied current-head proof, not a safe autonomous repair.

target.title: fix(ollama): bypass managed proxy for loopback embeddings
target.body:
## Problem

When `proxy.enabled=true`, OpenClaw routes strict HTTP fetches through the managed forward proxy. That is the right default for ordinary egress, but it breaks the bundled Ollama memory embedding provider when the configured Ollama `baseUrl` points at a host-local loopback service such as `http://127.0.0.1:11434`: a remote or policy-strict proxy cannot reach the OpenClaw host's loopback service.

PR #80751 made configured local provider origins trusted for SSRF policy purposes, but it did not change managed-proxy routing. PR #83255 addresses a separate browser/CDP loopback path. This PR covers the remaining Ollama memory embedding path.

## Change and value

This PR lets the Ollama memory embedding provider mark its exact configured loopback `baseUrl` as eligible for a guarded managed-proxy direct path. When the managed proxy is active, `fetchWithSsrFGuard` still resolves and validates the destination first, then uses a pinned direct dispatcher only when the request origin exactly matches the configured provider origin, the configured origin is loopback, and the resolved target addresses are loopback.

The practical effect is that host-local Ollama memory embeddings can work with `proxy.enabled=true` without creating a general local-network bypass. Public, LAN/private, and otherwise non-loopback Ollama origins stay on the managed proxy path.

## Who is affected, and who is not

Affected:

- Users running the bundled Ollama memory embedding provider against a loopback Ollama `baseUrl` while the managed proxy is active.
- Operators who need `proxy.loopbackMode` to keep explicit control over loopback behavior.

Not affected:

- Public Ollama Cloud or other public embedding endpoints: these stay on the managed proxy path.
- LAN/private non-loopback Ollama endpoints: these are not granted a direct proxy bypass by this PR.
- Browser/CDP loopback discovery and WebSocket routing: that is the separate #83255 scope.
- General user WebUIs, arbitrary local model servers, and other local-network targets: this does not add a broad bypass.
- Redirects away from the configured Ollama origin: those are re-evaluated and do not inherit the bypass.

## Why now

Issue #81249 still appears unresolved for users whose remaining failure is Ollama/local embeddings under the managed proxy. The existing fixes cover adjacent pieces, but not this transport decision. This PR is intentionally narrow so maintainers can review the Ollama embedding behavior separately from the browser/CDP change in #83255.

## Implementation

- Adds a `managedProxyBypass` marker to guarded fetch options for the specific `configured-local-origin` case.
- In managed-proxy strict mode, resolves the request hostname under the existing SSRF policy before deciding transport.
- Uses direct pinned dispatch only when the request origin exactly matches the configured origin, the configured origin is loopback, all resolved addresses are loopback, and `proxy.loopbackMode` allows it.
- Keeps public, LAN/private, and otherwise non-loopback configured origins on the managed proxy path.
- Honors `proxy.loopbackMode: "proxy"` by leaving loopback traffic on the proxy path.
- Honors `proxy.loopbackMode: "block"` by rejecting loopback provider traffic before opening a connection, including bracketed IPv6 loopback URLs.
- Switches the Ollama embedding SSRF policy from configured hostname trust to configured exact-origin trust.
- Documents the narrower Ollama memory embedding behavior in the provider and network proxy docs.

## Risks and review notes

This intentionally treats an exact configured loopback Ollama embedding origin as operator-trusted local IPC for the bundled embedding provider. That matches the local-provider direction from #80751, but it is still a proxy policy/API boundary and should get maintainer review.

This PR is still draft until final review gates are complete.

## Out of scope

- It does not change the generic model/chat provider transport path.
- It does not address browser/CDP loopback behavior; see #83255.
- It does not install or run a real Ollama daemon.
- It does not introduce a new public config knob.

## Verification

Remote GCP Spot validation (`us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`) from a fresh `openclaw/openclaw` clone at exact PR head `b2afedab26` / `b2afedab2657fb469eb2fa2441fc19345d017bd7`, Node v24.15.0:

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Proof harness with fake loopback Ollama `/api/embed` and fake managed proxy:
  - `direct exact-origin Ollama embedding bypass: ok`
  - `proxy.loopbackMode=proxy keeps Ollama embedding on proxy path: ok`
  - `redirect away from configured Ollama origin remains blocked: ok`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ssrf.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` (`68 tests passed`)
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm check:import-cycles`
- `pnpm build:strict-smoke`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm plugins:assets:build`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` (`208 files / 2755 tests passed`, including `extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.test.ts` with 20 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` (`58 files / 319 tests passed`)
- Full `pnpm test` was run remotely and exited red. Classification runs on GCP showed:
  - `src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`, and `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.forward-compat.errors-and-overrides.test.ts` reproduce with the same failures on `origin/main` at `57178b188b`.
  - `src/channels/plugins/configured-state.test.ts`, `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`, and `src/channels/plugins/contracts/plugin.registry-backed-shard-e.contract.test.ts` passed as targeted reruns on both PR head and `origin/main`; their full-suite red appearances look order/parallel related and unrelated to this patch.
  - `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` is flaky on both sides: PR head retry results were 2 fail / 1 pass; current `origin/main` retry results were 1 fail / 2 pass.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Ollama memory embeddings configured to a host-local loopback `baseUrl` should still work when `OPENCLAW_PROXY_ACTIVE=1` and `HTTP(S)_PROXY` points at a managed proxy that cannot reach host loopback. The same path should keep `proxy.loopbackMode=proxy` on the proxy path and should not trust redirects away from the configured origin.

**Real environment tested:** Remote GCP Spot VM (`us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`), Node v24.15.0, exact PR head `b2afedab26`. The proof starts a fake Ollama-compatible `/api/embed` service on `127.0.0.1:<random>` and a fake managed proxy on `127.0.0.1:<random>` that returns 503 for ordinary and CONNECT requests. It sets `OPENCLAW_PROXY_ACTIVE=1`, points `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` at the fake proxy, and clears `NO_PROXY`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** A remote proof harness imported `extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.ts`, created the real Ollama embedding provider with `remote.baseUrl` set to the fake loopback service, and executed three requests: default `gateway-only`, `proxy.loopbackMode=proxy`, and a cross-origin loopback redirect.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
direct exact-origin Ollama embedding bypass: ok
proxy.loopbackMode=proxy keeps Ollama embedding on proxy path: ok
[security] blocked URL fetch (memory-remote) targetOrigin=http://127.0.0.1:<redirect-port> reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
redirect away from configured Ollama origin remains blocked: ok
```

**Observed result after fix:** In default `gateway-only` mode, the real Ollama embedding provider code reached the fake host-local Ollama service directly, returned the normalized vector `[0.6, 0.8]`, and did not hit the fake proxy. With `proxy.loopbackMode=proxy`, the same request hit and failed through the fake proxy. A redirect from the configured origin to a different loopback port was blocked by the existing SSRF guard.

**What was not tested:** A real Ollama daemon/GPU was not installed. The proof covers the provider transport and response-handling path with an Ollama-compatible fake `/api/embed` endpoint and fake managed proxy, not actual local model execution.

Related #81249.
target.author: kaspre
